title: Neurolaena suriani
uses: Neurolaena suriani is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Neurolaena suriani has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may contribute to its medicinal effects. intro: Neurolaena suriana, commonly known as 'Mexican tea' or 'Mexican mint', is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in treating various conditions including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Neurolaena suriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are among the most common preparations used to harness its health benefits.
